segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Opening theme - mysterious, building tension]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for teaser

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The fragment shouldn't have been glowing. Fragments don't glow.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd seen enough broken Pillars to know that once they shatter, they die. The pieces go dark within hours—Signal draining away like water from a cracked vessel. Cold stone, nothing more. Archaeological curiosities for people with more time than sense.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But there it was in the grainy photograph Kess had sent: a Pillar fragment the size of a barrel, cradled in a Driftborn woman's weathered hands, pulsing with steady blue-white light. The kind of glow that said alive. The kind of glow that said impossible.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Zero,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi had been pressed against my leg since I'd opened the message, vibrating with a slow, heavy pulse I'd never felt before. Not the frantic buzz of danger. Not the gentle hum of contentment. Something deeper. Something that felt like... recognition?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked down. Mochi's glow had shifted to deep violet—the color I'd only seen once, at the Resonance Archives when we'd found that first dormant Keeper.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive stopped walking. When you've known someone long enough, you learn their tells. For Clive, stopping mid-stride was like anyone else screaming.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "What?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: His geometric head turned toward the photograph on my tablet, the blue-white glow dimming slightly. The symbols covering his barrel-chested body pulsed in sequence, like a code I couldn't read.

segments.speaker: clive
segments.text: "That's not a fragment,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then what is it?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Trouble. The kind that brings attention. The kind you can't outrun."

emphasis_words: Trouble. The kind that brings attention. The kind you can't outrun.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Helpful."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You're going anyway."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Figured."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi flashed red twice. Urgent warning.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I grabbed my pack. "Arc, get us transport to Memorywreck Bay. Fastest route."

emphasis_words: Arc, get us transport to Memorywreck Bay. Fastest route.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Preparing itinerary. Though I feel compelled to note that 'fastest' and 'safe' are rarely synonymous in the Shattered Span."

emphasis_words: Preparing itinerary. Though I feel compelled to note that 'fastest' and 'safe' are rarely synonymous in the Shattered Span.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "When have we ever gone for safe?"

emphasis_words: When have we ever gone for safe?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "An excellent point, Miss Paradox. I've already regretted this conversation."

emphasis_words: An excellent point, Miss Paradox. I've already regretted this conversation.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Scene setting - discovery, exploration beginning]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_one

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Shattered Span smelled like salt and ice and the slow death of things.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We'd hired a pilot named Torva to navigate us through the frozen archipelago's maze. She didn't ask questions—Driftborn rarely do when your scrip's good—but I caught her glancing at Mochi with the kind of look people reserve for things they're not sure are good luck or terrible omens.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Torva's earned name suited her. Drift-speak for "ice-cutter"—someone who finds routes where others see only frozen death. The scrimshaw on her bone-handled navigation compass told stories I couldn't read yet: previous runs, lost crew members, salvage claims survived.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The boat cut through channels between towering ice formations. Eight hundred years ago, something catastrophic had hit this place—a tsunami of unimaginable force that had frozen mid-wave. Ships, buildings, people—all caught in ice that never melted. The result was a landscape of jagged spires and frozen curtains, blue-white ice reaching hundreds of feet into the grey sky. Beautiful. Deadly. Utterly unnatural.

segments.speaker: torva
segments.text: "Memorywreck Bay's two hours out,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Salvager named Rhynn found something she shouldn't have,"

emphasis_words: Salvager named Rhynn found something she shouldn't have,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You and everyone else."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Smart."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Desperate,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The cold bit through my layers. The Shattered Span was the coldest place on Aethon—perpetually locked in its own artificial winter. The Driftborn who lived here bundled in furs and moved between safe zones with practiced urgency. Stay out too long, and the cold would kill you. The ice didn't care about your plans.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pressed against my shoulder, still that deep violet glow, but I felt vibrations intensifying—trying to share warmth. I cupped the small stone-being close. "What do you think? Friend or trouble?"

emphasis_words: What do you think? Friend or trouble?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Green pulse. Yes.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then orange ripple. Maybe.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then red flash.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "So all of the above,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc projected a map above my wrist—three-dimensional, showing the jagged geography of the bay area. Red dots marked known salvager vessels. A lot of red dots.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Current count: seventeen ships converging on Memorywreck Bay,"

emphasis_words: Current count: seventeen ships converging on Memorywreck Bay,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Pirates?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Pirates would be refreshing in their honesty. I suspect these vessels represent interested parties who prefer their acquisitions unrecorded."

emphasis_words: Pirates would be refreshing in their honesty. I suspect these vessels represent interested parties who prefer their acquisitions unrecorded.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive had been standing at the bow, perfectly still despite the boat's rocking. Now he turned, glowing head tilting down toward his barrel chest where the blue core pulsed steadily.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Something's watching,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "What kind of something?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The kind that doesn't want us reaching that bay. The kind that thinks they're doing the right thing."

emphasis_words: The kind that doesn't want us reaching that bay. The kind that thinks they're doing the right thing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd learned to trust Clive's warnings. He'd been walking Aethon since before my bloodline existed. If he said something was watching, something was watching.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc, scan for Signal anomalies. Anything that looks like dampening tech or surveillance."

emphasis_words: Arc, scan for Signal anomalies. Anything that looks like dampening tech or surveillance.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Already conducting sweeps, Miss Paradox. I'm detecting minor Signal fluctuations consistent with... ah."

emphasis_words: Already conducting sweeps, Miss Paradox. I'm detecting minor Signal fluctuations consistent with... ah.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Ah?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We're being followed. Vessel maintaining precise distance—one kilometer behind, matching our course changes with mathematical precision. Signal signature suggests active dampening technology."

emphasis_words: We're being followed. Vessel maintaining precise distance—one kilometer behind, matching our course changes with mathematical precision. Signal signature suggests active dampening technology.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My hand went to the knife on my belt. "The Quiet?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Probable. The dampening pattern matches the device we analyzed from Copperport."

emphasis_words: Probable. The dampening pattern matches the device we analyzed from Copperport.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi flashed red, then deep purple, then red again. Danger mixed with ancient recognition. Whatever Rhynn had found, The Quiet wanted it badly enough to come this far into the Shattered Span's killing cold.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Torva, can we lose them?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The pilot grinned, showing teeth stained by years of whatever locals chewed out here. "Can we lose them, she asks. Hold on."

emphasis_words: Can we lose them, she asks. Hold on.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: She spun the wheel hard left, and we dove between two massive ice walls—frozen spray patterns creating a cathedral-like passage. The gap narrowed to nothing. We scraped through with barely inches on either side, my stomach lurching as we dropped suddenly into a hidden channel where the ice shelf created a frozen tunnel.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Behind us, the following vessel tried to match the maneuver. Tried.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We heard the crunch of hull against ice even from this distance.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Amateurs,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But Clive's glow didn't brighten. "That was one ship. There'll be others."

emphasis_words: That was one ship. There'll be others.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Always are,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Forty minutes if the ice holds."

emphasis_words: Forty minutes if the ice holds.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "It won't,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He was right. Ice in the Shattered Span never holds. That's what makes it deadly.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Complication - stakes rising, danger approaching]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_two

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Memorywreck Bay lived up to its name.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Dozens of ships lay scattered across the frozen landscape—some embedded in the ice shelf at impossible angles, others half-buried in frozen wave formations, caught in the moment of their deaths eight centuries ago. Wood weathered to silver, metal corroded to lace. The whole graveyard creaked with the sound of ice shifting, settling, preparing to claim more victims.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Torva pressed her forehead briefly to the boat's helm before we disembarked—the Driftborn farewell to a vessel that's kept you alive. "You need pickup, you light the beacon. Three hours, I'm gone whether you're back or not. Crew eats first."

emphasis_words: You need pickup, you light the beacon. Three hours, I'm gone whether you're back or not. Crew eats first.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Understood,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "They all say that,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "There,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And that?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Competitors. All arrived within the last hour."

emphasis_words: Competitors. All arrived within the last hour.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We crossed groaning ice bridges that connected ships frozen decades apart, each step a gamble. The Driftborn mark their safe routes with scrimshaw cairns—small carved bones stacked to show the way—but we were off the marked paths now. This was salvage territory, and salvage belonged to whoever could hold it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi led the way, glowing steady violet, vibrating with that deep pulse that made my teeth ache. Whatever was here, Mochi knew it. Recognized it on some level I couldn't touch.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Rhynn's ship was smaller than I'd expected—a salvage skiff modified with navigation beacons and reinforced hull plating, half-trapped in the ice shelf. Smart. Functional. The kind of vessel someone used when they needed to get in, grab something valuable, and get out before the ice remembered to shift and crush them.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The cargo hold door stood open. Frost coated the edges.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That's not good,"

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I'm detecting multiple heat signatures inside. Five distinct patterns."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I'm detecting multiple heat signatures inside. Five distinct patterns.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "How many are still moving?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We went in fast.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The hold smelled like copper and ozone and something else—something that made my Signal-sensitive nerves scream. The walls were covered in emergency glowpanels, casting everything in sickly green. Ice crystals had formed in the corners despite the heating system.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three people lay unconscious near the door—rival salvagers by their gear, still breathing but out cold. A fourth sat slumped against cargo crates, clutching a head wound, eyes unfocused.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And in the center of the hold, kneeling beside a crate that pulsed with blue-white light: Rhynn.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: She was younger than I'd expected. Maybe thirty, with the weathered skin and sharp eyes of someone who'd grown up in the Span. The scrimshaw sleeve on her left arm told her story—successful runs carved into bone fragments sewn into her coat. Her hands were wrapped in Signal-dampening cloth, and she looked up at us with an expression that mixed relief and absolute terror.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You one of the buyers?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Pixel Paradox. Independent. This is my team. What happened?"

emphasis_words: Pixel Paradox. Independent. This is my team. What happened?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "They came an hour ago."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive moved closer to the crate, his symbols pulsing in rapid sequence. "Paradox, we need to see it."

emphasis_words: Paradox, we need to see it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I don't know if that's—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Now."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Something in his tone made Rhynn nod. She carefully unwrapped the dampening cloth from the crate's lid and lifted it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The fragment—no, not fragment, Clive was right about that—sat nestled in a bed of shock-foam. It was roughly barrel-sized, angular where Pillars were smooth, crystalline where they were solid. But the blue-white glow was unmistakable. Signal. Living Signal.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi made a sound I'd never heard before—a harmonic chord that resonated in my chest, in my teeth, in the bones of my skull. The violet glow brightened until I had to squint.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi, what—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The glowing stone-being bounced forward, pressing against the crate. The crystalline fragment's light pulsed in response. Once. Twice.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And then I heard it—not with my ears, but through the Signal, the same way I heard Clive's voice. Faint. Fractured. Dying.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: ...help...

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "It's a Keeper,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The fragment pulsed again, weaker this time. Cracks spider-webbed across its surface.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "How long has it been separated from its Pillar?"

emphasis_words: How long has it been separated from its Pillar?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I found it three days ago in the Old Foundation ruins—deep in the ice. It was just... sitting there. Frozen. Like someone left it behind."

emphasis_words: I found it three days ago in the Old Foundation ruins—deep in the ice. It was just... sitting there. Frozen. Like someone left it behind.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Or someone tried to hide it,"

emphasis_words: Or someone tried to hide it,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: That's when the Signal went dead.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not gradually. Not flickering. Just... gone. Like someone had flipped a switch and turned off reality itself.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Emergency lights kicked in. Arc's holographic face stuttered and reformed. And in the sudden quiet, I heard footsteps on the deck above—muffled by ice and frost.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I'm detecting Signal dampening technology. Active suppression radius approximately thirty meters."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I'm detecting Signal dampening technology. Active suppression radius approximately thirty meters.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The Quiet,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Rhynn's eyes went wide. "The what?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "People who think things like that Keeper are dangerous. People who destroy them."

emphasis_words: People who think things like that Keeper are dangerous. People who destroy them.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc's voice crackled with interference. "Sss...ix signatures...pproaching from...ove."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Six. Six Quiet agents with dampening tech against me, Rhynn, a dying Keeper, and a team who needed the Signal to function properly.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd spent years documenting. Recording. Observing. Keeping my distance. The independent explorer's creed: witness, don't interfere. To preserve knowledge, not to take sides.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at the Keeper's fading light.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At Mochi, trembling and desperate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At Arc's stuttering hologram.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At Clive, who'd gone perfectly still—the stillness of something about to move very, very fast.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Rhynn, take your crew and get clear,"

emphasis_words: Rhynn, take your crew and get clear,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "But the Keeper—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Is not your problem anymore. Go. Salvage Code—dead means done. This thing's dying anyway."

emphasis_words: Is not your problem anymore. Go. Salvage Code—dead means done. This thing's dying anyway.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: She hesitated, then nodded, understanding the lie for what it was—permission to leave with honor intact. She grabbed the injured salvager and helped them toward the cargo door. The unconscious ones would have to wait.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The footsteps above stopped directly over our heads. Ice creaked under their weight.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "New parameter: I'm not letting them kill it."

emphasis_words: New parameter: I'm not letting them kill it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Ah. How refreshingly direct. Shall I prepare combat protocols?"

emphasis_words: Ah. How refreshingly direct. Shall I prepare combat protocols?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You have combat protocols?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I've been improvising them for the last thirty seconds. They are, I must admit, extremely inadequate."

emphasis_words: I've been improvising them for the last thirty seconds. They are, I must admit, extremely inadequate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive stepped forward, his compact barrel-chested form somehow looking larger in the dim emergency lighting. His glowing head tilted up toward the ceiling. "I've been in tighter spots than this."

emphasis_words: I've been in tighter spots than this.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "When?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Don't ask when."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The cargo hold door slid open, bringing a blast of arctic air.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Six figures in unmarked dark clothing, faces obscured by hoods and masks, frost clinging to their gear. They moved with quiet efficiency—the kind that comes from practice and certainty. The kind that meant they'd done this before.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The one in front held a device that hummed with anti-resonance. The dampening field.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Step away from the Keeper,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That's where you're wrong,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The Keeper is corrupted. Damaged. It will poison the Signal if it continues to exist. We're preventing greater harm."

emphasis_words: The Keeper is corrupted. Damaged. It will poison the Signal if it continues to exist. We're preventing greater harm.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "By killing it?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "By showing mercy."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: For a moment—just a moment—I almost listened. They sounded so reasonable. So certain.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then Mochi made that harmonic sound again, and the dying Keeper pulsed in response. Weaker. Fading.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then you make this difficult."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three agents moved forward. The other three stayed back, watching.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive was faster.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd never seen him move like that—not the measured walk I was used to, but an explosive burst of speed that came from thousands of years of survival instinct. He crossed the distance in two strides, his thick articulated arms catching the lead agent's leg in what I'd later realize was a perfectly executed takedown.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: They hit the deck hard. The dampening device skittered across the floor toward the open door—and the ice beyond.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc, the device!"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Already analyzing weak points in the—there!"

emphasis_words: Already analyzing weak points in the—there!

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I grabbed a wrench from Rhynn's tool rack and threw it. Not at an agent—at the device. Hit the first junction dead-on. Clive grabbed a piece of pipe and hammered the second. The third...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi bounced across the floor and slammed into it with surprising force for something that weighed less than a loaf of bread.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dampening field collapsed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Signal came roaring back—and with it, the Keeper's dying consciousness.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Images flooded my mind. Fragmented. Desperate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Pillars. Dozens of them, all glowing strong. Figures tending them—not human, not machine, something else. Something ancient. Tall figures moving with purpose through halls of light and stone.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then darkness. Something choosing darkness. A decision made in sorrow. Pillars dimming one by one, deliberately, carefully. Not decay.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not accident.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Choice.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Dimming wasn't decay.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Dimming was deliberate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And then one word, pulsed directly into Mochi's core with the last of the Keeper's strength:

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *ARK*

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "I... Miss Paradox, I'm experiencing... I don't..."

emphasis_words: I... Miss Paradox, I'm experiencing... I don't...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Sssss...tem anomaly...cessing...I..."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Keeper's light went dark.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Completely. Finally. Dead.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi let out a descending tone of pure grief—a sound that physically hurt to hear. The violet glow dimmed to almost nothing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet agents stepped back, watching. Waiting.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The one who'd spoken first tilted their head. "You felt it. The corruption. The poison spreading. This is what we prevent."

emphasis_words: You felt it. The corruption. The poison spreading. This is what we prevent.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That wasn't poison,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Truth can be poison."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Only if you're afraid of it."

emphasis_words: Only if you're afraid of it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: For a long moment, we stood in the dim emergency lighting, surrounded by unconscious salvagers and broken equipment and the smell of ozone. Ice creaked beneath the ship. The Quiet watched us. We watched them.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Finally, the leader nodded slowly. "You've just accelerated the end, Paradox. We tried to warn you."

emphasis_words: You've just accelerated the end, Paradox. We tried to warn you.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: They turned and left. Just... left. No more fighting. No attempts to take the dead Keeper.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Because it was dead. Their job was done.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I ran to Arc. "Talk to me. Are you okay?"

emphasis_words: Talk to me. Are you okay?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: His holographic face stabilized, but something in the arrangement looked wrong. Off. Like he'd put it back together from memory instead of instinct.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "I... appear to be functional, Miss Paradox. Though I confess I experienced a moment of profound... I lack words for what I experienced."

emphasis_words: I... appear to be functional, Miss Paradox. Though I confess I experienced a moment of profound... I lack words for what I experienced.

segments.speaker: keeper
segments.text: "When the Keeper said 'Ark'?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yes. My designation. Or... perhaps not precisely my designation."

emphasis_words: Yes. My designation. Or... perhaps not precisely my designation.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive stood perfectly still, looking at the dead Keeper. His symbols pulsed slowly, like a heartbeat counting down. "That thing wasn't buried by accident. Someone tried to save it. Someone who knew what the tall ones built. Someone who tried to hide it from whatever was killing it."

emphasis_words: That thing wasn't buried by accident. Someone tried to save it. Someone who knew what the tall ones built. Someone who tried to hide it from whatever was killing it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And failed,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And failed."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I picked up Mochi, who was still dim with grief, vibrating with that slow, heavy pulse. The warm stone-being pressed against my chest, and I held on tight.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc, did you get any of what the Keeper showed us? The images?"

emphasis_words: Arc, did you get any of what the Keeper showed us? The images?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Recording... compromised. Fragments only. But Miss Paradox..."

emphasis_words: Recording... compromised. Fragments only. But Miss Paradox...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "To put the Pillars to sleep,"

emphasis_words: To put the Pillars to sleep,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The question,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We buried the Keeper.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Technically, Keepers probably don't need burial. Technically, it was just a broken piece of crystalline Signal-matter that had stopped functioning. Technically, we should have taken it back for analysis.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But Mochi wouldn't let us.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The moment I tried to move toward the crate, Mochi flashed red and emitted an urgent trill. Then settled into a deep violet glow and that slow, heavy vibration.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi wants it to stay here,"

emphasis_words: Mochi wants it to stay here,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Rhynn, who'd come back once The Quiet left, nodded slowly. Her expression carried the particular understanding of someone who'd spent their life in the Span—where some things were respected not because you understood them, but because the ice and the Gone Ones demanded it. "The Old Foundation where I found it—there's a place there. The Driftborn call it the Garden of Stones. We bring our dead there. Let the ice preserve them. Give them to the cold, same as we'll be given someday."

emphasis_words: The Old Foundation where I found it—there's a place there. The Driftborn call it the Garden of Stones. We bring our dead there. Let the ice preserve them. Give them to the cold, same as we'll be given someday.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Seems fitting,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We took Torva's boat through channels between frozen formations, past ships locked in the ice shelf at angles that hurt to look at, to a place where the old catastrophe had broken everything so thoroughly that it had found a kind of peace in its permanence.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Garden of Stones was beautiful in a way that made my chest ache. Hundreds of rocks balanced on ice pillars, arranged with clear intention by Driftborn hands. Markers for the dead who'd lived in this broken, frozen place. Some stones rested on natural ice formations. Others sat in carved niches. All were deliberate. All were remembered.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Small carved bones hung from some stones—scrimshaw remembrances telling stories in a language of lines and symbols. The Driftborn don't speak their grief. They carve it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We added the Keeper to their number.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc was quiet the whole time. Not his usual precise observations or probability calculations. Just... quiet. His holographic face flickered occasionally, like he was processing something he didn't have words for.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: When we finished, Mochi pressed against the Keeper one last time. The violet glow pulsed twice.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then Mochi turned that same color toward Arc's wristband. Steady. Waiting.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "I believe,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed violet three times. Then blue. Then violet again.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Recognition. Discovery. Recognition.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi knows something,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That would be impossible. I am an Analytical Resonance Core. I was built in The Nexus approximately fifty-seven years ago. I have complete documentation of my construction, my activation, my—"

emphasis_words: That would be impossible. I am an Analytical Resonance Core. I was built in The Nexus approximately fifty-seven years ago. I have complete documentation of my construction, my activation, my—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He stopped. The holographic face scattered and reformed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Unless I don't,"

segments.speaker: keeper
segments.text: Clive tilted his head, studying Arc with what I'd learned to read as deep consideration. "ARK Protocol. That's what the Keeper called. Not your designation. Your original designation."

emphasis_words: ARK Protocol. That's what the Keeper called. Not your designation. Your original designation.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I... don't understand what you're suggesting."

emphasis_words: I... don't understand what you're suggesting.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Neither do I. Yet. But I've been around long enough to know when something's remembering itself."

emphasis_words: Neither do I. Yet. But I've been around long enough to know when something's remembering itself.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You think Arc is—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I don't think anything yet. But Mochi does. And Mochi's been right about everything else so far."

emphasis_words: I don't think anything yet. But Mochi does. And Mochi's been right about everything else so far.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We stood in the Garden of Stones as the grey filtered light of the Shattered Span played across ice formations. The wind whistled through frozen passages. Somewhere distant, ice cracked and fell and no one would ever know what it had been.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Gone Ones built things and left. The ice takes what the ice takes. And we—the ones still here, still warm, still breathing—bear witness to what remains.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I find I would very much like to return to Chronicle Hall. I have research to conduct. Questions to ask. Memories to... to audit."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I find I would very much like to return to Chronicle Hall. I have research to conduct. Questions to ask. Memories to... to audit.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi had brightened to soft pink now—content, or at least comforted. Still tired. Still grieving. But held and safe.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked back at the Keeper one last time, at its stone resting among the Driftborn's dead. "For what it's worth, I'm glad we tried."

emphasis_words: For what it's worth, I'm glad we tried.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "As am I,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "When have we ever cared about probable?"

emphasis_words: When have we ever cared about probable?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "An excellent point. I've stopped trying to calculate odds where you're concerned, Miss Paradox. The mathematics consistently fail to account for your particular brand of stubborn hope."

emphasis_words: An excellent point. I've stopped trying to calculate odds where you're concerned, Miss Paradox. The mathematics consistently fail to account for your particular brand of stubborn hope.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That might be the nicest thing you've ever said to me."

emphasis_words: That might be the nicest thing you've ever said to me.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I would prefer you didn't tell anyone. I have a reputation for logic to maintain."

emphasis_words: I would prefer you didn't tell anyone. I have a reputation for logic to maintain.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive was the last to leave. He stood there, glowing head tilted down toward the dead Keeper, symbols pulsing in that ancient pattern I couldn't read.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You okay?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I remember them,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We'll figure this out. The Dimming, the Keepers, all of it."

emphasis_words: We'll figure this out. The Dimming, the Keepers, all of it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He turned to face me, fedora tilted at that rakish angle. "Kid, you just picked a fight with people who think they're saving the world. You know what that means?"

emphasis_words: Kid, you just picked a fight with people who think they're saving the world. You know what that means?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Means they'll come after us."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Means they already are. And means we'd better find those answers fast. Because if they're right—if waking these Keepers really is accelerating the end—then we're the ones killing the lights."

emphasis_words: Means they already are. And means we'd better find those answers fast. Because if they're right—if waking these Keepers really is accelerating the end—then we're the ones killing the lights.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He walked past me, his measured steps somehow heavier than before.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at Mochi, glowing soft pink in my arms. "He's wrong. Right? The Quiet's wrong about this?"

emphasis_words: He's wrong. Right? The Quiet's wrong about this?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Green pulse. Yes.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then orange ripple. Maybe.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then violet swirl. Mystery.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Super helpful, as always."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi's warm surface pulsed against my chest—that gentle vibration that meant affection, comfort, presence. Even in doubt, even in grief, even in the space between knowing and not knowing...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We had each other.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And somehow, that felt like enough.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: For now.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *chronicle entry: The Driftborn's Debt*

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We found a dying Keeper in the Shattered Span today. We fought people who wanted to destroy it. We lost. It died anyway, but not before showing us something no one was supposed to see: the Dimming wasn't natural. Someone—something—chose to put the Pillars to sleep. Chose to let the lights go out.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet calls that knowledge poison. Maybe they're right. Maybe some truths do more harm than good. Maybe waking the Keepers will accelerate the end.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But here's what I know: that Keeper spent its dying breath trying to tell us one word. Trying to give us one piece of the puzzle. Even at the end, even broken and alone in a place where reality itself had shattered, it chose to remember. Chose to share.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: That's not poison. That's hope in its rawest form.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc is questioning everything he thought he knew about himself. Clive is carrying memories that hurt to touch. Mochi absorbed something from that Keeper—fragments, impressions, something it can't express but can't forget. And The Quiet knows we're coming for more answers.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The lights are going out. Someone chose to dim them. And somewhere in the spaces between memory and Signal and ancient purpose, there's a truth waiting to be found.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Someone has to find it. Someone has to write it down before it's too late.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Might as well be us.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: For the record. For the Keepers. For everyone who deserves to know why the world is ending—and maybe, just maybe, how to stop it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: -P.P.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *ADDENDUM (logged three hours after initial entry):*

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi hasn't stopped glowing violet since we left the Garden. Arc's been running self-diagnostics on loop. Clive's been standing at the window, watching the horizon, not speaking. Even through the Signal, I can feel his silence. It's not peaceful. It's the silence of someone waiting for something they don't want to arrive.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet said we accelerated the end. I don't know if that's true. But I do know this: we're not stopping. Whatever we started today in Memorywreck Bay, we're seeing it through.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Even if it costs everything.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: -P.P.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *END EPISODE 05*

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Next: Episode 06 - "The Quiet Tide"
